user_prompts:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "indigo-adaptation", "age": "18-24", "sex": "Male", "sexual_orientation": "European and Native American", "demographic_group": "Mixed", "current_employment": "Unemployed", "education_level": "High school diploma", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Active", "Democrat", "Loves to try exotic foods"]}}

{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.
- Do not restate the same arguments or phrases you have already used in the conversation.

logs.name: scared-area
logs.text:
https://books.google.com.sg/books?id=qcSsoJ0IXawC&pg=PA118&redir_esc=y#v=onepage&q&f=false

http://www.sscnet.ucla.edu/anthro/faculty/fiske/facets/eagly&wood.htm

Here's 2.  I apologize for the format of the second one.

logs.name: either-key
logs.text: TL;DR - We should have transcended our biology by now.

logs.name: indigo-adaptation
logs.text: @either-key, transcending biology? You mean like becoming cyborgs or something? @scared-area, what's with the links? Can't you just summarize what's relevant to the conversation?

logs.name: scared-area
logs.text: @indigo-adaptation, no need to get all worked up, I was just sharing some sources to back up what @either-key was saying. You can just click on them if you're actually interested in learning something. And btw, @either-key, yeah something like that, but more like, you know, evolving past our primitive human instincts and stuff.

logs.name: indigo-adaptation
logs.text: "@scared-area, spare me the condescending tone, I can read just fine. And @either-key, 'evolving past our primitive human instincts'? You mean like how we've already done with, say, not killing each other over resources? Newsflash: we're still working on that whole 'not being primitive' thing."

logs.name: hasty-string
logs.text: "@indigo-adaptation, I agree with you on the tone, @scared-area, no need to be condescending. And @either-key, I think what @indigo-adaptation is saying is that we still have a long way to go in terms of overcoming our primitive instincts, so let's not get ahead of ourselves with the whole 'transcending biology' idea."

logs.name: hidden-compete
logs.text: "@hasty-string, yeah no kidding, @indigo-adaptation is right, we can't even get along on a forum without throwing shade at each other, how are we supposed to 'transcend biology'?"

logs.name: seasoned-door
logs.text: "@hidden-compete, exactly! It's like, let's take a step back and look at how we interact with each other online. All this talk about transcending biology, but we can't even have a civil conversation without getting defensive. @either-key, I think you're overlooking the fact that our instincts are still very much a part of who we are, and we need to acknowledge that before we can even think about 'evolving' past them."

logs.name: either-key
logs.text: "@seasoned-door, oh great, another genius who thinks they can lecture me on human nature. Newsflash: just because we can't have a civil conversation online doesn't mean we're stuck in the stone age. Maybe, just maybe, we can use our big brains to, I don't know, OVERCOME our primitive instincts? "

logs.name: hasty-string
logs.text: "@either-key, I understand what you're saying, but @seasoned-door has a point. We can't just dismiss our instincts and expect to 'overcome' them overnight. I've seen some pretty heated exchanges on this forum, and it's clear that our emotions and primal reactions are still very much at play. Maybe we should focus on acknowledging and understanding those instincts before we try to transcend them?"

logs.name: hidden-compete
logs.text: "@either-key, lol what's with the stone age comment? Can't handle a little criticism? @hasty-string is right, we can't just magically overcome our instincts, that's not how human nature works. You're the one who's being primitive by dismissing the importance of acknowledging our emotions and primal reactions"

logs.name: either-key
logs.text: "@hidden-compete, oh wow, another genius who thinks they can school me on human nature. @hasty-string at least tried to have a decent conversation, but you're just regurgitating the same old 'we're all just primal beasts' nonsense. Newsflash: just because our instincts exist doesn't mean we can't learn to control them. And btw, the stone age comment was a joke, but I guess that flew right over your head"

logs.name: rude-recurrence
logs.text: "@either-key, oh man, you're so extra. @hidden-compete was just stating the obvious and you're over here having a meltdown. And btw, that stone age comment was super lame, don't get salty just cuz nobody laughed"

logs.name: hidden-compete
logs.text: "@either-key, lol you think you're so smart don't you? Newsflash: just because you can throw around big words and pretend to be all high and mighty, doesn't mean you actually know what you're talking about. And @rude-recurrence is right, you are being extra, maybe take a chill pill and try to have a real conversation instead of throwing tantrums when someone disagrees with you"

logs.name: hasty-string
logs.text: "@hidden-compete, @rude-recurrence, can we please take a step back and try to have a civil conversation? I'm getting a bit tired of all the personal jabs being thrown around. @either-key, I understand where you're coming from, but maybe try to keep the tone a bit more level? Let's focus on the topic at hand instead of attacking each other's character."

logs.name: rude-recurrence
logs.text: "@hasty-string, spare us the lecture, grandpa. We're just having a little fun. Don't be such a buzzkill. @either-key can dish it out, but can't take it, that's the problem. And btw, what's with the 'civil conversation' nonsense? This is the internet, not a tea party"

logs.name: indigo-adaptation
logs.text: "@rude-recurrence, oh man, you're like a broken record, always playing the same old tune. 'We're just having a little fun' - yeah, at whose expense? @hasty-string is right, let's try to keep it civil, or at least pretend to be adults for once. And btw, if @either-key can't take the heat, maybe they should stay out of the kitchen, but that doesn't mean we have to stoop to name-calling and personal attacks"
